ServiceDefinitions.Definitions: Wells constructed as a source of potable water for individual households
Wells constructed for use as regulated public water supplies.
Wells constructed for use in commercial business activities.
Wells constructed for use in space heating or cooling, or other applications involving the use or transfer of groundwater heat. For wells greater than 500 feet, refer to DEC permitting requirements.
Wells constructed for irrigation of farmland or as a water supply for livestock.
Wells constructed to monitor the physical or chemical character of groundwater.
Wells constructed to characterize subsurface geology or for dewatering during construction activity.
Wells constructed for the use of placing fluid underground. Permits for injection wells are obtained from the US EPA Underground Injection Control program.
Wells constructed for irrigation of properties such as homes and golf courses.
Wells or boreholes constructed or used for the sole purpose of lowering or controlling the level of groundwater.
Permanent closure of a well by physically removing the well from service.
Control of a water well such that water no longer flows beyond the well casing.
Installation of new water well pumps.
Service of existing water well pumps.

Introduction and circulation of a carefully determined amount of an approved disinfection solution to a water well for treatment of bacteria. Additional information is available from your local Health Department.
The process where fluid is pumped down a well at high pressures to open fractures in adjacent rock to improve water flow to the well.
Physical or chemical process intended to removed sediment, scale, or other material that may be interfering with the amount of water available for pumping.
